questions.question: President of India can resign from the office by addressing the resignation letter to who among the following?
questions.explanation: The President holds office for a term of five years from the date on which he enters upon his office. However, he can resign from his office at any time by addressing the resignation letter to the Vice-President.

questions.question: The date of election of speaker of Lok Sabha is fixed by whom among the following?
questions.explanation: Whenever the office of the Speaker falls vacant, the Lok Sabha elects another member to fill the vacancy. The date of election of the Speaker is fixed by the President.

questions.question: In case of arbitrary detention, which among the following writs can be issued by Supreme Court?
questions.explanation: Habeas Corpus writ is a bulwark of individual liberty against arbitrary detention. It would set the detained person free, if the detention is found to be illegal.

questions.question: In order to initiate a resolution for the removal of Speaker of legislative assembly, notice has to be issued how many days prior to such initiation?
questions.explanation: To remove a speaker, a resolution need to be passed by a majority of all the then members of the assembly. Such a resolution can be moved only after giving 14 days advance notice.

questions.question: Which among the following authorities can summon the houses of parliament?
questions.explanation: The president from time to time summons each House of Parliament to meet.

questions.question: District judges are appointed by ___________?
options: Judge nominated by Chief Justice of High Court
Minister responsible for Law and Order
questions.explanation: The appointment, posting and promotion of district judges in a state are made by the governor of the state in consultation with the high court.

questions.question: What is the term of office to the panchayats at village level?
questions.explanation: The Panchayat Raj act provides for a five-year term of office to the panchayat at every level.

questions.question: Which among the following type of urban governments are created for administration of big cities?
questions.explanation: Municipal corporations are created for the administration of big cities like Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Hyderabad, Bangalore and others.

questions.question: Which among the following is NOT a reason for creation Union Territories?
questions.explanation: The union territories have been created for a variety of reasons. 1. Political and administrative consideration 2. Cultural distinctiveness 3. Strategic importance 4. Special treatment and care of the backward and tribal people.

questions.question: Election Commission is NOT responsible to hold elections to which among the following Offices?
questions.explanation: Article 324 of the Constitution provides that the power of superintendence, direction and control of elections to parliament, state legislatures, the office of president of India and the office of vice-president of India shall be vested in the election commission.

questions.question: To remove a member of UPSC on the grounds of Misbehavior, which among the following authorities is responsible for holding an enquiry?
questions.explanation: Removal of the chairman or any other member of UPSC for misbehavior has to be referred to the Supreme Court for an enquiry.

questions.question: A qualified person can be appointed as Additional Judge of High Court for what maximum period?
questions.explanation: A qualified person can be appointed as additional judges of a high court for a temporary period not exceeding two years.

questions.question: Which among the following High Courts doesn't have original civil jurisdiction in cases of higher value?
questions.explanation: The four high courts (i.e., Calcutta, Bombay, Madras and Delhi High Courts) have original civil jurisdiction in cases of higher value.

questions.question: Which among the following courts can issue writs for purposes other than fundamental rights?
questions.explanation: Article 226 of the Constitution empowers a high court to issue writs including habeas corpus, mandamus, certiorari, prohibition and quo-warrento for the enforcement of the fundamental rights of the citizens and for any other purpose.

questions.question: Part IV of the constitution is NOT applicable to which state?
questions.explanation: Part IV (dealing with Directive Principles of State Policy) and Part IVA (dealing with Fundamental Duties) are not applicable to the state of Jammu & Kashmir.

questions.question: Constitution provides special provisions for totally how many states?
questions.explanation: Articles 371 to 371-J in Part XXI of the constitution contain special provisions for eleven states viz., Maharashtra, Gujarat, Nagaland, Assam, Manipur, Andhra Pradesh, Sikkim, Mizoram, Arunachal Pradesh, Goa and Karnataka.

questions.question: The ideal of Liberty in the constitution was taken from which country?
questions.explanation: The ideals of liberty, equality and fraternity in our Preamble have been taken from the French Revolution.

questions.question: Original Constitution provided for how many fold of classification of states?
questions.explanation: In 1950, the Constitution contained a four-fold classification of the states of the Indian Union - Part A, Part B, Part C and Part D State.

questions.question: Before attaining full-statehood, which among the following states was categorized as an "autonomous state"?
questions.explanation: The 22nd Constitutional Amendment Act (1969) created Meghalaya as an 'autonomous state' or 'sub-state' within the state of Assam with its own legislature and council of ministers.

questions.question: A person who has rendered distinguished service to which field will NOT be considered for granting citizenship through naturalization process?
questions.explanation: Government of India can grant citizenship through naturalisation in the case of a person who has rendered distinguished service to the science, philosophy, art, literature, world peace or human progress.

questions.question: Which among the following articles doesn't deal with Right to Freedom of Religion?
questions.explanation: Right to freedom of religion includes Articles from 25 to 28. Article 29 deals with right of minorities.

questions.question: Provision of Directive Principles conflicts with which provision of constitution?
questions.explanation: The justiciability of Fundamental Rights and non-justiciability of Directive Principles on the one hand and the moral obligation of State to implement Directive Principles (Article 37) on the other hand have led to a conflict between the two since the commencement of the Constitution.

questions.question: Which authority must pass a resolution to empower Parliament to legislate on state list?
questions.explanation: The Parliament is empowered to legislate on any subject of the State List if Rajya Sabha passes a resolution to that effect in the national interest.

questions.question: President can reduce the period of sentence of a prisoner with which of the following powers?
questions.explanation: Remission implies reducing the period of sentence without changing its character. The pardoning power of the President is independent of the Judiciary; it is an executive power.

questions.question: The council of ministers is collectively responsible to _________?
questions.explanation: Article 75 states that the council of ministers is collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha.

questions.question: Which is regarded as continuing chamber?
questions.explanation: The Rajya Sabha (first constituted in 1952) is a continuing chamber, that is a permanent body and not subject to dissolution.

questions.question: The 73rd Amendment Act relating to Panchayat is applicable to which among the following states?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment act does not apply to the states of Jammu and Kashmir, Nagaland, Meghalaya and Mizoram.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of members who can constitute Central Vigilance Commission?
questions.explanation: The CVC is a multi-member body consisting of a Central Vigilance Commissioner (chairperson) and not more than two vigilance commissioners.

questions.question: Which among the following languages is NOT one among the list of languages to be added to Eighth Schedule?
questions.explanation: Bodo, Dongri, Maithili and Santhali were added by the 92nd Amendment Act of 2003 to the eighth schedule. Nepali was added prior to this.

questions.question: Governor can nominate how many members of Anglo-Community to the state legislative assembly?
questions.explanation: The governor can nominate one member from the Anglo-Indian community, if the community is not adequately represented in the assembly.
